primary_metric: % of home-page visitors who select an audience lens (Dev or PM) — engagement with the new core wayfinding mechanism
success_metrics: Lens-selection rate among home visitors ≥ target (T1, GA4 home_lens_select / home page_view; baseline + target set in Phase 1 PRD)
Home bounce-rate reduction vs pre-redesign baseline (T1, GA4)
Case-study reach depth improves — more sessions expand/open studies older than the newest era (T1, GA4 case_study era-expand + study open events)
kill_criteria: Lens-selection rate below floor AND home bounce rate worsens vs baseline after 30 days → revert to a neutral (lens-less) home
Lens switching causes measurable confusion — support/feedback signals OR a drop in cross-page navigation depth vs baseline
kill_criteria_resolution: Pre-registered, not yet evaluable at ship. The lens mechanism is net-new (baseline 0) and both kill criteria are 30/60-day metric evaluations against GA4. First review 2026-07-09 (T+30d); primary-metric (lens-selection rate >=40%) evaluation 2026-08-09 (T+60d). No kill condition is triggerable at ship time; resolution recorded at the first review.
dispatch_pattern: agent-driven (cross-repo: state.json + spec in FitTracker2, implementation in fitme-story)
scope_summary: Reorients the fitme-story public site around TWO audiences — developers and product managers — via a persistent, cookie-backed, server-rendered 'lens' (dev|pm) switchable from the header. The lens defines the NARRATIVE SPINE: in the PM lens the product/process story (pm-flow, lifecycle, design system, outcomes) leads and engineering supports; the Dev lens flips it (architecture, gates, state schema, dev-guide, Code Connect lead). Rebuilds the home page (compact: who/what + origin hook + early lens chooser + numbers + 3 featured studies; drops the 4-persona ThreeWaysIn, keeping only Dev+PM) and adds a lens-aware /story narrative page (who → what → how it started → how it grew → today). Reworks /case-studies into an era-grouped collapsible layout (newest era expanded; subject sub-groups) backed by new category/era frontmatter on the 68 MDX files. Adds the missing v7.9.1 entry to the framework timeline. OUT OF SCOPE: /control-room (gated operator surface), the iOS app, and authoring any NEW case-study content. /about is retained alongside the new /story.

research.notes: Brainstorm complete (design locked via 4 structured decisions: persistent lens switch, Dev+PM only, era-grouped collapsible case studies, compact home). Operator refinements: keep /about alongside /story; PM lens makes pm-flow + design-system first-class story chapters with dev side supporting. research.md + design spec authored + approved (spec reviewed in IDE).

ux_or_integration.notes: ux-spec.md authored, grounded in real fitme-story symbols (preflight pass). Key reconciliation: narrow existing PersonaProvider 4→2 + add cookie SSR layer; reuse Disclosure/CaseStudyCard/Tag/Stat/Button; one new primitive (LensToggle). Web repo — iOS ui-audit/AppTheme gates N/A; compliance checked vs design-tokens.ts + ui/. Analytics: no consent gate (GA env opt-in) — T15 becomes no-op-guard test. T14-T16 need test+lighthouse harness bootstrap (+1.0d).

review.notes: fitme-story #213 CI all-green (lighthouse, verify, audit, gates, mdx-render, unit-tests, Vercel, GitGuardian); dual pre-merge review (ux + design) passed.

merge.notes: Implementation squash-merged in fitme-story (#213, commit 1f5bd09). FT2 side carries the PM paper trail (state + case study) merged separately to FT2 main; pr_number left null (cross-repo dual-outlet: the runnable merge is the fitme-story PR).

documentation.notes: Case study at docs/case-studies/fitme-story-dual-audience-redesign-case-study.md. Showcase MDX (fitme-story/content/04-case-studies/) is a fitme-story-repo follow-up per chronological-order rule.

tasks.title: getLens() server util + fitme_lens cookie contract + default resolution
tasks.note: src/lib/lens.ts — server-only Lens type, LENS_COOKIE, getLens()/getLensWithDefault() (async cookies()), isLens guard, PM default. Additive; does not yet narrow Persona. Shipped (with all tasks) via fitme-story #213 squash-merge.

tasks.title: Lens persistence + provider wiring (cookie mirror in persona.ts; PersonaProvider hoisted in layout; client-resolved to keep site static — cacheComponents off)
tasks.note: Deviation from spec's SSR-layout-read: cacheComponents is OFF, so a root-layout cookie read would force the whole static site dynamic (perf-guardrail regression). Lens resolves client-side (consistent with existing persona/theme); cookie added for persistence + per-page server opt-in (lens.server.ts getLens). Flagged for operator review.

tasks.note: Lens-typed layer over persona context; LensGate defaults to PM when unset.

pre_merge_review.ux_note: Shipped surfaces match ux-spec: LensToggle (radiogroup a11y), compact home chooser, era-grouped /case-studies, lens-aware /story + framing. 5 states covered (static pages; empty/error fallbacks in case-study list + featured).

transitions.note: Design spec approved (reviewed in IDE). GA4 home baseline pulled: 76.2% bounce.
PRD approved. 16 tasks across 7 groups; lens engine is the foundation dependency.
Tasks approved. UX spec grounded in real symbols; lens = refactor of existing PersonaProvider (4→2) + cookie SSR layer.
UX approved. Implementing in fitme-story; T9 v7.9.1 quick win first, then lens-engine refactor.
CI green incl. 3 new test files; self pre-merge review next.
Dual pre-merge review (ux+design) passed; fitme-story #213 CI all-green + mergeable. User approved merge.
Case study + state reconciled. Feature complete; first metric review 2026-07-09 (T+30d).

persona_narrowing_decision: SUPERSEDED (2026-06-09): full Persona 4->2 type narrowing + dead-component prune NOT done. Reason discovered in implementation: PersonaBar + PersonaIndicator are catalogued design-system components (src/lib/design-system.ts with githubPath) AND carry Code Connect .figma.tsx mappings AND persona_emphasis data carries hr/academic keys. The persona substrate must stay 4-valued to back those retained showcase components + data. The two-audience guarantee is enforced at the LENS layer (only dev|pm are settable via LensToggle, readable via cookie, surfaced in nav/home/story/case-studies). ThreeWaysIn no longer rendered (left in repo; no per-action deletion approval). Net: lens-on-persona is the correct architecture, not a shortcut.
